user_message: Help me prep the QBR preparation for Iron Creek Mining. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 11:29 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 16:11 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
